intro: Nesaea lanceolata, commonly known as 'Water Hyssop', is a perennial aquatic plant native to tropical Africa, characterized by its lance-shaped leaves and purple flowers. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which can aid in reducing fever, relieving respiratory issues, and protecting against infections. The bioactive constituents of Nesaea lanceolata include flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Nesaea lanceolata is commonly prepared into teas, infusions, and decoctions, as well as dried and used in capsule or tablet form, to leverage its therapeutic benefits.
